statement.markdown: Complete the function to enqueue elements into the queue. The function should handle both single and multiple elements at once.
statement.inputFormat: A list of integers or a single integer representing the element(s) to be enqueued.
statement.outputFormat: The modified queue after enqueuing the element(s).
statement.notes: Note that the queue is initially empty and can store integers.

constraints.description: Number of elements to be enqueued

examples.explanation: Enqueue three elements into the queue
Enqueue a single element into the queue

hints.text: Pay attention to the case when the input is a list of elements.

editorial.title: Correct Broken Queue Enqueue
editorial.intuition: Think of how you would manually add elements to the end of a queue.
editorial.bruteForce: You can use a loop to add each element individually.
editorial.optimalSolution: Utilize the deque class with append functions to add elements efficiently.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run will be provided in the solution.
commonMistakes: Incorrectly handling single and multiple elements
